heading: Pensioners, indigent patients, and persons who die on military reservations
label: 1484 Pensioners, indigent patients, and persons who die on military reservations
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)14845:2156 (as applicable to armed forces).(as applicable to armed forces), .July 15, 1954, ch. 507, § 668 Stat. 479The words “If proper disposition of the remains cannot otherwise be made” are substituted for 5:2156 (last sentence). The words “maintained and” and “incurred for”, and the words “articles of” in clause (3), are omitted as surplusage. The words “of that department” are inserted for clarity.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)14845:2156 (as applicable to armed forces).(as applicable to armed forces), .July 15, 1954, ch. 507, § 668 Stat. 479The words “If proper disposition of the remains cannot otherwise be made” are substituted for 5:2156 (last sentence). The words “maintained and” and “incurred for”, and the words “articles of” in clause (3), are omitted as surplusage. The words “of that department” are inserted for clarity.

children.text: notification to the next of kin or other appropriate person;
preparation of the remains for burial, including cremation;
furnishing of a casket or urn, or both, with outside box;
transportation of the remains to a cemetery selected by the Secretary; and
